{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Bezold-Jarisch reflex is described in Document [2] as decreasing heart rate (bradycardia) by inhibiting the sympathetic drive and causing hypotension and peripheral vasodilation. However, Document [17] discusses the Bainbridge reflex, which is similar in that it involves baroreceptors and autonomic nervous system regulation. It mentions that the Bezold-Jarisch reflex works by inhibiting sympathetic drive, which aligns with the action of histamine in causing vasodilation and hypotension.
